User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user in the SeniorComex application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The email address of the user.

role.description: The role of the user (Administrator, Operator, Finance, Logistics).

AuditLog.title: AuditLog
AuditLog.description: Represents an audit log entry for changes made in the application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AuditLog entry.

userId.description: Reference to the User who made the change. (Relationship: User 1:N AuditLog)

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the change was made.

entityType.description: The type of entity that was changed (e.g., 'Product', 'Order').

entityId.description: The ID of the entity that was changed.

oldValue.description: The old value of the field that was changed. JSON formatted.

newValue.description: The new value of the field that was changed. JSON formatted.

Report.title: Report
Report.description: Represents a report generated by the application.

dateGenerated.description: The date and time the report was generated.

fileUri.description: The URI where the report file is stored.

userId.description: Reference to the User who generated the report. (Relationship: User 1:N Report)

definition.description: Stores user profiles. The 'userId' parameter identifies each user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Stores audit logs for each user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: auditLogId
params.description: The unique identifier for the audit log entry.

definition.description: Stores reports generated by each user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: reportId
params.description: The unique identifier for the report.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to support the SeniorComex application's requirements, focusing on secure access, audit trails, and reporting. The structure emphasizes Authorization Independence by denormalizing access control information where necessary and uses structural segregation to simplify security rules. It prioritizes clarity, predictability, and ease of maintenance.

**Authorization Independence:**
*   The `AuditLog` and `Report` subcollections under `/users/{userId}` denormalize the `userId` to establish clear ownership. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls to parent documents to verify user identity, making the security rules simpler and more robust.
*   Role-based access control relies on path-based ownership (`/users/{userId}`) and dedicated collections (e.g. `/roles_admin/{uid}`) to avoid complex rule logic and authorization dependencies.

**Structural Segregation:**
*   User-specific data (AuditLogs, Reports) are stored under their respective `/users/{userId}` paths, separating it from other user data. This allows for specific security rules to be applied to a user's data without affecting other users.

**QAPs Support:**
*   List operations are secured through path-based rules. Listing audit logs or reports is only allowed for the user who owns the data, preventing unauthorized access. The path structure `/users/{userId}/auditLogs` enables this secure listing. The same applies to `/users/{userId}/reports`.

**Additional Notes:**
*   The structure is designed to be scalable, allowing for a large number of users and associated data. The use of subcollections helps to organize data and simplifies querying.
*   Semantic naming conventions are used throughout the structure to improve readability and maintainability. Wildcards are descriptive (e.g., `{userId}`), and entity names are consistent.

This design ensures that security rules are simple, robust, and easily debuggable, adhering to the core design principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC (Database-Based Access Control), and QAPs (Rules Are Not Filters).
